title: quercus calliprinos var. integrifolia
intro: Quercus calliprinos var. integrifolia is a variant of the Quercus calliprinos plant, a type of evergreen oak tree native to the Mediterranean region. It is also known as the 'Evergreen Oak' or 'Cypriot Evergreen Oak', characterized by its small, leathery leaves and edible acorns. The leaves and bark of Quercus calliprinos var. integrifolia have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including diarrhea, fever, and skin conditions, and is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
